text: This is the VOA Special English Technology Report.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语科技报道。

text: A team of scientists in the United States has created a new kind of mouse
zh: 美国一组科学家培育出了一种新型小鼠，

text: that has an immune system similar to that of humans.
zh: 它的免疫系统与人类免疫系统相似。

text: The scientists hope their research with these mice will speed up development
zh: 科学家们希望，通过对这种小鼠的研究，

text: of a vaccine to prevent human immunodeficiency virus or HIV.
zh: 能加快预防人类免疫缺陷病毒（即艾滋病病毒，HIV）疫苗的研发。

text: Scientists from the Reagan Institute of Massachusetts General Hospital,
zh: 来自麻省总医院里根研究所、

text: Massachusetts Institute of Technology and Harvard are carrying out the new research.
zh: 麻省理工学院和哈佛大学的科学家们正在开展这项新研究。

text: Earlier research has shown that certain individuals
zh: 此前的研究已经表明，

text: with HIV have immune systems that do better at controlling the AIDS virus.
zh: 某些感染HIV的人，其免疫系统在控制艾滋病病毒方面表现更好。

text: These individuals are commonly known as elite controllers.
zh: 这些人通常被称为“精英控制者”。

text: They often live longer with the virus and have fewer problems early on.
zh: 他们往往能带毒生存更长时间，早期出现的问题也较少。

text: Todd Allen is one of the lead authors of the new study,
zh: 托德·艾伦是这项新研究的主要作者之一，

text: which was published in Science Translational Medicine.
zh: 该研究发表在《科学转化医学》杂志上。

text: He says some people are able to control HIV very well.
zh: 他说，有些人能够很好地控制住HIV。

text: By using the new experimental mice,
zh: 通过使用这种新的实验小鼠，

text: the researchers hope to learn what it is about the immune systems
zh: 研究人员希望弄清楚这些“精英控制者”的免疫系统

text: of these elite controllers that causes them
zh: 究竟有什么特别之处，

text: to deal with the HIV virus better than others.
zh: 使他们比其他人更能有效应对HIV病毒。

text: The humanized mice were created using stem cells and tissue from human donors.
zh: 这种“人源化”小鼠是利用干细胞和来自人类捐献者的组织培育而成的。

text: Some of the tissue was taken from the liver and thymus.
zh: 部分组织取自肝脏和胸腺。

text: The thymus is a large gland at the bottom of the throat.
zh: 胸腺是位于喉咙底部的一个较大的腺体。

text: It trains T lymphocytes, or T cells, to attack unwelcome microbes.
zh: 它训练T淋巴细胞（即T细胞）去攻击有害微生物。

text: In that way, it protects the body from infection.
zh: 由此，它保护人体免受感染。

text: When the scientists infected the so -called humanized mice with the HIV virus,
zh: 当科学家们给这些所谓的“人源化”小鼠感染HIV病毒后，

text: the T cell reaction in the mice was exactly the same as that of humans.
zh: 小鼠体内的T细胞反应与人体的反应完全相同。

text: Earlier research using rhesus monkeys helped scientists understand how the virus attacks cells.
zh: 早期利用恒河猴进行的研究帮助科学家们了解了病毒攻击细胞的方式。

text: These monkeys were seen as good replacements for humans
zh: 这些猴子曾被视为人类的良好替代模型，

text: because they could easily be infected with
zh: 因为它们很容易感染上

text: the primate version of HIV known as SIV.
zh: 灵长类版本的HIV病毒，即猴免疫缺陷病毒（SIV）。

text: But genetic differences in the two versions of the virus and the immune systems
zh: 但两种病毒版本及免疫系统之间存在的基因差异，

text: suggested that monkeys were not the best candidates for HIV research.
zh: 表明猴子并非HIV研究的最佳对象。

text: Todd Allen says the experiments with the new humanized mouse
zh: 托德·艾伦表示，利用新型人源化小鼠进行的实验

text: did a better job of showing what happens in the human body with the AIDS virus.
zh: 更好地展示了艾滋病病毒在人体内的实际作用过程。

text: He says the experimental mouse permits researchers
zh: 他说，这种实验小鼠让研究人员

text: to test the discoveries they have made about individuals infected
zh: 能够验证他们此前在感染HIV的个体身上所发现的规律。

text: with HIV. Todd Allen and the other researchers hope further studies
zh: 托德·艾伦和其他研究人员希望，进一步的研究

text: with the humanized mouse will lead to an HIV vaccine.
zh: 借助这种人源化小鼠，最终能推动HIV疫苗的问世。

text: For VOA Special English, I'm Carolyn Persutti.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语，我是卡罗琳·佩尔苏蒂。
